reviews.review_msg: Amazing prof and really opens up students' perspectives and challenges ways of thinking. Participation is a must in the course, but if you try and really get into the thinking it's not a hard class at all. Lectures are great - can ruin your favourite movies tho.

Such an amazing prof! I search for classes of his to take again because he is so passionate.  I find I learn a lot from him even though he is tough.  He expects a lot of participation and well organized responses.  He makes you work for your grade.

The prof made this class really unenjoyable, she sat in her chair the entire lecture and read off her notes/was really unengaging during her lectures. She makes the assignments more difficult and stressful because of her lack of communication and unclear expectations. She constantly contradicts herself, and lacks compassion for students.

This was my first GSWS class and I enjoyed it, my enjoyment was due to the course material/ content entirely and unrelevant from the prof. The prof answered everything by saying "read the syllabus". The lectures are quite dry and she can be seen mostly just reading off a script or printed piece of paper; would not want her as my prof ever again.

Literally love Dr. Faris so much! I have had about 4 or 5 classes with her as they often fit into my schedule the best. She is always so wonderful and the topics are so interesting. Lots of special topic courses so keep an eye out for possible classes and the title as they change often. She is amazing at giving in depth feedback.

I regret taking her class. Her lesson plans are mixed up. she gives 10 minutes break to students than she herself her 5 min cant control herself to start the class even though she is seeing the students are eating. Also attendence is super mandatory, she hates students who does not go to her class and end up giving them 0.
Lectures were super boring - just read off a script. Tough grader and not very clear with what is required
